source: Attack on Titan by Hajime Isayama

active_desires.desire: That the freedom survives — that his friends live long enough to use it.
One more conversation with Armin and Mikasa without the weight of what he knew.
The ocean. Just standing there without any soldiers on the other side.

blind_spots.soul_belief: I did what was necessary. They are alive and free. That's what it cost.
blind_spots.actual_driver: The people he killed also had their own Armins and Miksasas. The 'justification' works only because he drew the circle of people-who-count around Paradis. The circle was drawn arbitrarily and he knows it.

blind_spots.soul_belief: I saw the path. I walked it because there wasn't another one.
blind_spots.actual_driver: He spent very little time exploring paths that didn't end in him doing something. His action-bias shaped which paths he saw as viable. He does not examine this.

blind_spots.soul_belief: I asked them to stop me. I wanted someone to find another way.
blind_spots.actual_driver: He designed the situation so that stopping him required killing him, which required his friends to carry the weight of his death forever. He's not sure whether this was love or the cruelest thing he ever did to Mikasa.
